items.title: Security, human rights top Asean topics during 59th AMM and related meetings
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippines is set to lead critical discussions on regional security, human rights, and geopolitical stability as it hosts the 59th Asean Foreign Ministers' Meeting (AMM) and Related Meetings from July 20 to 24, the Philippine News Agency (PNA) reported. In a press briefing on Monday, Foreign Affairs Assistant Secretary and Asean Spokesperson Dominic Xavier Imperial said the high-level gathering will focus on advancing key development priorities and reinforcing the regional bloc's community identity amid rising global tensions. The week-long activities commenced with the 16th face-to-face interface between Asean Foreign Ministers and representatives of the Asean Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights (AICHR).

items.title: PN, BN machinery cooperation smooth in Negri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) campaign machinery have been operating smoothly, with grassroots coordination remaining positive, Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said. Tuan Ibrahim, who is also PN deputy chairman, said most campaign activities were focused on each candidate's respective constituencies, although several joint programmes involving BN and PN were also being carried out. Despite the conditions, Tuan Ibrahim said ceramah continued to draw encouraging crowds.

items.title: Court: Zizie had no knowledge of Felcra investment, acquitted
items.summary: Judge Rosli Ahmad said the defence had established that Zizie had no knowledge of Felcra Berhad's RM150 million investment in Public Mutual unit trust products and was never involved in her late husband's official duties as Felcra's former non-executive chairman. In his broad grounds of judgment, Rosli said the defence relied on the evidence of Zizie, Bung Moktar and former Felcra group chief financial officer Mohd Azmy Abu Samad. The accused never asked the deceased about Felcra Berhad's affairs.

items.title: Court told RM8mil transferred from FashionValet to 30 Maple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd's bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018. CIMB Bank's Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch customer service manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple's bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet's account. "On Aug 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, 'REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE'.

items.title: Timeline on Zizie Izette RM2.8mil corruption abetment case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Actress-turned-businesswoman Datin Seri Zizie Izette A. Samad was acquitted earlier today of three charges of abetting her late husband Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin in receiving RM2.8 million in bribes linked to a RM150 million unit trust investment by Felcra Bhd. Sept 2, 2022 : Sessions Court finds prosecution has established a prima facie case and orders Bung Moktar and Zizie to enter their defence. Sept 7, 2023 : High Court overturns Sessions Court's ruling, acquitting both.

items.title: Last of Mexico's narco old guard, 'El Mayo' faces life in jail
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, founding leader of the legendary Sinaloa cartel, will be sentenced in New York on Monday, a ruling expected to send Mexico's last old-school druglord to prison for life. After a journey from poor farmworker to one of the world's most powerful and wealthy criminal bosses, Zambada was arrested at a US airport in July 2024. Mexico's drug violence has surged since then.

items.title: Sarawak’s jet age begins as Abang Johari joins AirBorneo maiden lift‑off to Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUCHING, July 20 — AirBorneo today made history with the commencement of its first jet operations through the inaugural Kuching-Kuala Lumpur flight. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg was among the passengers on the maiden flight, MY5005, which departed from Kuching International Airport at 1.20pm for Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA). Like Sarawak, Japan is an island so connectivity is extremely important.

items.title: Indian police baton charge protesters marching to parliament
items.summary: NEW DELHI: India's riot police baton-charged protesters in New Delhi as they tried to march to parliament on Monday, escalating a protest over irregularities in major examinations. Protesters ran for cover as police in blue riot gear charged at them near the Jantar Mantar protest site, an AFP journalist saw. Thousands of protesters started gathering from early Monday, defying a steady monsoon drizzle and police barricades put up on routes leading to the site.

items.title: Ryanair quarterly profit slumps by a third on lower fares, higher fuel costs
items.summary: DUBLIN: Ryanair's profit slumped by a third on higher fuel costs and weaker fares in the three months to the end of June, the airline said on Monday, while summer fares looked set to fall amid consumer nervousness around the Iran war and broader economy. At its last quarterly results in May, Ryanair said fares could be broadly flat between July and September. CFO SEES CAPACITY FALLS, FARE INCREASES IN COMING YEAR Weakness in fares is likely to be short-lived, however, as European aviation is facing a wave of consolidation and airline failures that will take out capacity, chief financial officer Neil Sorahan said.

items.title: Sessions Court hears RM8m FashionValet transfer, Vivy Yusof and husband deny breach of trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd’s bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018. CIMB Bank’s Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch Customer Service Manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple’s bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet’s bank account. “On August 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, ‘REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE’.

items.title: Cops probe fatal four-vehicle crash, father riding with three children
items.summary: The 37-year-old man and his children, aged 9, 7 and 5, sustained minor injuries after their motorcycle collided with a Perodua Alza. The crash, which occurred at 7.15am, involved a Honda Civic, a van, the Alza and the motorcycle. "The Honda, travelling from Permas City towards Kota Masai, collided with a van driven by a 39-year-old man.

items.title: Sabah receives RM600m interim grant, urges Putrajaya to release remaining RM900m this year
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 20 — Sabah has received RM600 million of the RM1.5 billion interim special grant from the federal government, with the balance of RM900 million requested to be paid within the year, said state Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun. Masidi, who is also Deputy Chief Minister II, said the RM1.5 billion payment announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is an interim arrangement and does not represent the final amount due to Sabah under the Federal Constitution. Other allocations include RM278 million for operational expenditure, RM210 million for investments, RM162 million for administrative expenses, RM93 million for domestic grants and RM13 million under special provisions.

items.title: Cyprus marks 52 years since invasion as UN renews peace push
items.summary: NICOSIA: Cyprus marked the 52-year anniversary of Turkiye's 1974 invasion on Monday as the Greek and Turkish Cypriot communities prepared for a renewed effort from the United Nations to break years of deadlock in peace talks. Air raid sirens sounded over the Southern Greek Cypriot government-controlled areas of Cyprus on Monday morning to commemorate the hour Turkish forces landed in 1974, days after a short-lived Greek-inspired coup. Greek Cypriots seek reunification as a federation, while Turkiye and the Turkish Cypriot leadership seek two sovereign states.

items.title: MN Holdings secures SC approval for Main Market transfer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MN Holdings Bhd has received the Securities Commission's approval to transfer its listing from the ACE Market to the Main Market of Bursa Malaysia. Managing director Datuk Clement Toh described the move as a significant milestone for the group, reflecting its stronger financial performance and expanded project portfolio. Toh said the transfer puts MN Holdings in a stronger position to capitalise on Malaysia's continued investment in power infrastructure, data centres, renewable energy, water, gas and utilities-related developments.

items.title: 'Sometimes when it touches conscience, I speak out,' says Anwar
items.summary: KAJANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has apologised if his tone while reprimanding civil servants came across as harsh. Anwar said he had often been advised to speak more calmly and engage with the public in a more approachable manner after assuming the post of Prime Minister. "You have to speak calmly, speak well and engage with the people.

items.title: Lab tests confirm Selangor ICU patient free of leptospirosis, health dept dismisses viral claims
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 20 — Laboratory tests have confirmed that a patient admitted to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of a hospital in Selangor on July 7 with suspected leptospirosis did not contract the disease, the Selangor State Health Department (JKNS) said today. “Of the total, 26 cases, or 21 per cent, were linked to recreational water activities,” said the statement, which was issued following the circulation of social media messages claiming a leptospirosis case had originated from a recreational water area in Selangor. It said three recreational water sites in Selangor frequently associated with leptospirosis cases are Taman Eko Rimba Sungai Kanching in Gombak, Sungai Congkak Waterfall in Hulu Langat and Sungai Ulu Yam in Hulu Selangor.

items.title: Sonam Wangchuk, Indian activist rallying Gen Z against Modi
items.summary: INDIAN social activist Sonam Wangchuk may be pushing 60, but he has become the face of a Gen Z movement demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's education minister over exam paper leaks, drawing wider support after he was forcibly taken to hospital following three weeks of fasting. Wangchuk, who is widely believed to have inspired the lead character in a Bollywood blockbuster, is best known for campaigning on issues affecting his Himalayan region of Ladakh, bordering China. "I am no Gandhi, no hero.

items.title: Malaysia welcomes Zhouzhuang partnership, pledges stronger cultural ties and heritage conservation with China
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Malaysia today welcomed a tourism and heritage cooperation initiative with China’s century-old Zhouzhuang Ancient Town, aimed at strengthening cultural exchange, sustainable tourism and heritage conservation between both countries. Ye Xin, general manager of Kunshan Water Town Zhouzhuang Cultural Tourism Development Group Co. Limited, said China and Malaysia share a common cultural heritage and a long history of cultural exchanges. “Malaysia’s thriving tourism industry and abundant outbound travel market make it a key overseas market for Zhouzhuang.

items.title: 'Eight years is too long': Anwar orders review of promotion delays
items.summary: BANGI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered a review of civil service promotion processes after receiving complaints that some officers had to wait up to eight years for advancement. Anwar said the directive followed feedback from civil servants during engagement sessions held nationwide, where concerns were raised over lengthy delays affecting certain service grades. He said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar had informed him that the issue would be discussed at the next meeting to ensure promotions could be expedited.

items.title: Penang Port key logistics gateway to Thai border
items.summary: JELEBU: Penang Port continues to play an important role as a logistics gateway for southern Thailand by handling container and cargo movements between the neighbouring country and Malaysia, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said the port is handling a large number of containers to and from southern Thailand, thus strengthening its role as a regional logistics hub. "We do handle a lot of containers to and from Thailand, so Penang Port is now a gateway to southern Thailand.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Smoked duck traders eye sales surge in Kuala Pilah
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Traders selling smoked duck in Kampung Terachi are hoping the two-week campaign period for the Negri Sembilan election will bring a surge in business. Smoked duck trader Khairul Hafiq Kamarul Zaman, 26, said sales typically surge in the three days leading up to polling day, based on the trend observed during the 2023 state election. He said his smoked ducks were priced between RM45 and RM48 each.

items.title: US strikes Iran for ninth day, Iran immobilises two oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: DUBAI: US forces hit Iran for a ninth consecutive day on Monday as concerns grew over shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after Iran said two oil tankers had exploded and been immobilised. In a statement, US Central Command said it had begun "a new wave of strikes" aimed at "degrading" Iran's ability to attack commercial vessels in the strait's vital shipping lanes. Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency, citing its own reporters, said US missiles struck several Iranian cities early on Monday.

items.title: Tengku Ampuan Pahang offers land in Kuala Wau for displaced Orang Asli community
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Tengku Ampuan Pahang has expressed concern over the land dispute involving the Orang Asli community of Kampung Sungai Cot and offered a new settlement on her land in Kuala Wau, Maran. However, the offer was not specifically intended for the families of Kampung Sungai Cot, but was extended to other Orang Kita (Orang Asli) families requiring shelter or relocation, subject to Her Royal Highness's consent. "Her Royal Highness has expressed deep sympathy and concern over the land dispute affecting the Orang Kita community.

items.title: Govt ready to take measures to protect people's welfare, economy amid geopolitical tension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is prepared to take the appropriate measures to safeguard the people's welfare and sustain the country's economic growth following the renewed escalation of geopolitical conflict in West Asia, said Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah. Hence, Mohd Shahar said, the government is closely monitoring the situation as it has the potential to put pressure on industrial production costs and the prices of goods, particularly if the conflict persists. "The government has mobilised various strategic and integrated intervention measures based on four key approaches: ensuring supplies, prolonging supply availability, curbing price increases, and capitalising on available opportunities.

items.title: Prysmian signs €5.5 billion Molex deal in data centre push
items.summary: ROME: Italian cable maker Prysmian said on Monday it had signed a long-term agreement worth up to €5.5 billion (US$6.4 billion) with Molex, a Koch-owned electronics company, as it stepped up its push into the fast-growing data centre market. The agreement is part of a broader set of deals and commercial initiatives with hyperscalers and data centre infrastructure providers. To support demand driven by AI infrastructure and data centre upgrades, Prysmian will more than double fibre capacity in the US The company will invest €1.25 billion through 2031 to expand optical cable and fibre production in the US and Europe.

items.title: Palm oil gains on surging crude prices, El Niño worries
items.summary: JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Monday, tracking a rally in crude oil pricesand strength in Dalian vegetable oils, while renewed worries about El Niño also helped pull prices higher. "BMD CPO futures opened gap higher following bullish rally in energy prices and in CBOT Soy oil futures from Friday and in Chinese vegetable oil futures in Asian hours today. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract gained 0.51 per cent, while its palm oil contract rose 1.16 per cent.

items.title: South Korea slides as AI selloff deepens; Gulf tensions cloud Asia outlook
items.summary: Shares in Seoul fell as much as 5.1 per cent as trading resumed after a market holiday on Friday. Taiwan's tech-heavy index rose as much as 1 per cent before paring gains, while the MSCI EM Asia equities index edged 0.5 per cent lower. The South Korean and Taiwanese benchmark gauges together account for about 60 per cent of the MSCI equities index.

items.title: Cops debunk viral e-hailing kidnap claim
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Police have dismissed a viral social media claim alleging an e-hailing driver attempted to abduct a female passenger here, saying investigations found no evidence of any criminal offence. "The e-hailing driver, who is in his 40s, lodged a police report on July 15 to deny the allegation and assist in the investigation. The allegation circulated widely on social media, including WhatsApp and Facebook, warning the public about an e-hailing driver who was accused of attempting to abduct a female passenger.

items.title: Immigration officer pleads not guilty to seven bribery charges totalling RM1,950
items.summary: MELAKA, July 20 — A Melaka Immigration Department officer pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court here today to seven charges of accepting bribes totalling RM1,950 last year. Mohd Nazrul Md Yasin, 39, entered the plea before Judge Mohamad Kamil Nizam. The foreign national, identified as Kusmiyati, was allegedly staying at the premises, an offence under the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: Govt cannot simply lower fuel prices further with RM40bil subsidy bill, says Anwar
items.summary: KAJANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended the government's fuel pricing policy, saying Putrajaya already spends about RM40 billion annually on subsidies and cannot reduce pump prices further without jeopardising the country's fiscal position. Anwar said calls for lower fuel prices often overlooked the scale of subsidies already borne by the government. "People ask why we do not reduce fuel prices further.

items.title: Man killed as car crashes into oncoming tanker in Kulai
items.summary: KULAI: A 45-year-old driver was killed after his car veered into the opposite lane and collided head-on with an oncoming tanker along Jalan Johor Baru-Ayer Hitam last night. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said the car entered the opposing lane before colliding with the oncoming tanker. The tanker driver escaped unhurt.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia's debt ratio improving despite inherited financial burdens
items.summary: KAJANG: Malaysia's debt-to-gross domestic product (GDP) ratio is improving despite the government continuing to shoulder billions of ringgit in legacy liabilities inherited from previous administrations, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Some say the government's debt is increasing. He said the government continued to spend almost RM1 billion annually servicing Felda's financial obligations alone.

items.title: Sweeping storm alert issued, MetMalaysia says treacherous weather threatens peninsula and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in Peninsular Malaysia and Sarawak until 5pm today. MetMalaysia announced that the affected areas are Kuala Lumpur, Perlis, Penang, Melaka as well as in Kedah involving the areas of Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Kulim and Bandar Baharu as well as Perak in Manjung, Central Perak, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak and Muallim. Other states also affected are Negeri Sembilan in Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin as well as Johor in Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat and Johor Bahru.

items.title: MCMC regulates platform risks, not algorithms directly, Teo Nie Ching tells Dewan Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) does not directly regulate or determine the design of platform algorithms, instead adopting a risk-based regulatory approach that places responsibility on licensed service providers to manage their own systems. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the approach requires licensed service providers to assess and manage the impact of their systems, including taking reasonable measures to reduce the algorithmic amplification of unlawful or harmful content. “MCMC adopts a risk-based regulatory approach that places the responsibility on licensed service providers to assess and manage the impact of their systems.

items.title: Heriot-Watt honours PNB Merdeka CEO Izwan Hasli, former Chief Justice Tengku Maimun
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heriot-Watt University Malaysia (HWUM) has awarded Honorary Doctor of the University (DUniv) degrees on former Chief Justice Tengku Tun Maimun Tuan Mat and Datuk Izwan Hasli Mohd Ibrahim, chief executive officer of PNB Merdeka Ventures, during its 2026 graduation ceremony. The university said the awards recognise their contributions to nation building, integrity and sustainable development. The ceremony also celebrated the graduation of 309 students across two sessions.

items.title: Mechanic jailed 20 years, caned for raping, robbing teen jogger in Tumpat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A mechanic who assaulted, raped and robbed a 15-year-old girl while she was out jogging in Tumpat last month was sentenced to 20 years’ jail and seven strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today. For the first charge, he was accused of raping the 15-year-old girl after causing injuries to her. For the second charge, Muhammad Faryzuan was accused of robbing the victim of her Vivo Y19 mobile phone while armed with a deadly weapon — a wooden stick.

items.title: Mechanic jailed 20 years, ordered seven strokes of the rotan for raping, robbing teen
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A 23-year-old mechanic was sentenced to 20 years' jail and seven strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to raping and robbing a 15-year-old girl in Tumpat last June. The charge was framed under Section 376(2)(a) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of up to 20 years and whipping. The court sentenced him to 15 years' jail and five strokes of the rotan for rape, and five years' jail with two strokes of the rotan for robbery.

items.title: Father fined RM3,000 after exposing toddler to drugs
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A labourer was fined RM3,000 by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to exposing his 1-year, 8-month-old daughter to dangerous drugs, causing the toddler to test positive for methamphetamine and benzodiazepines. Mohamad Shahrul Hisham Lailatulazhma, 27, entered the guilty plea before Judge Zulkpli Abdullah. The court may also order the offender to execute a bond for good behaviour.

items.title: Tekun disburses RM36mil to 1,500 entrepreneurs in Negri Sembilan as of June
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Tekun Nasional has disbursed financing totalling RM36 million to about 1,500 entrepreneurs in Negri Sembilan as of June this year. Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong said today that, at the national level, RM540 million has been disbursed to 25,600 entrepreneurs during the same period. According to Sim, Tekun, established 30 years ago, has contributed significantly to the development of local entrepreneurs.

items.title: Woman’s body found with hands, legs tied in Kota Tinggi river
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A woman’s body was found floating in Sungai Tanjung Serindit, Ulu Papan, Pengerang, Kota Tinggi last night, shocking residents after the victim was discovered with both her hands and legs tied with rope. The unidentified woman’s body was found after police received information from members of the public at about 9pm, Kosmo! Kota Tinggi district police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said a team from the Kota Tinggi district police headquarters and the Johor police contingent’s forensic unit rushed to the scene after receiving the report.

items.title: Economy Ministry: Govt readying measures as Middle East conflict pushes up oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The government is prepared to take the appropriate measures to safeguard the people’s welfare and sustain the country’s economic growth following the renewed escalation of geopolitical conflict in West Asia, said Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah. Hence, Mohd Shahar said, the government is closely monitoring the situation as it has the potential to put pressure on industrial production costs and the prices of goods, particularly if the conflict persists. “The government has mobilised various strategic and integrated intervention measures based on four key approaches: ensuring supplies, prolonging supply availability, curbing price increases, and capitalising on available opportunities.

items.title: PwC tapped to chart Penang’s roadmap for international financial hub, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 20 — The Penang state government has appointed PricewaterhouseCoopers Advisory Services Sdn Bhd (PwC) as the consultant to prepare the white paper, framework and action plan for the proposed Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC). Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said this decision was made by the PIFC special task force committee. He said PwC was given 20 weeks to complete the white paper, strategic framework (blueprint) and action plan as well as set the direction for the establishment of the PIFC.

items.title: Sungai Bakap Tamil school project gets 2027 deadline after long delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The construction project for Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (Tamil) (SJKT) Sungai Bakap, Penang, which has been delayed since 2015, is expected to be fully completed in the fourth quarter of 2027. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the project was continued after the Prime Minister approved a ceiling cost of RM12,667,367.59 with Yayasan Didik Negara appointed as the implementing agency. However, the Sessions Court dismissed the case filed against the MOE in May 2026,” she said.

items.title: ‘Who will pay for it?’ Anwar defends Malaysia’s fuel policy, warns against piling on debt
items.summary: KAJANG, July 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has defended the government’s fuel pricing policy, saying Malaysia’s ability to keep petrol prices at RM1.90 per litre comes despite the country still importing nearly half of petroleum requirements. Responding to criticism saying he had failed to reduce fuel prices, Anwar said the government had to consider the long-term financial burden of subsidies and existing debts inherited from previous administrations. Anwar said while some may call for further reductions in fuel prices, the government must consider how such measures would be financed.

items.title: Two arrested after drone smuggling attempt foiled at Melaka’s Sungai Udang Prison
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Prisons Department has foiled an attempt to smuggle prohibited items into the Sungai Udang Prison Complex in Melaka using a drone. The department said the suspicious activity was detected through security monitoring and control measures carried out by prison authorities before a police report was lodged for further enforcement action. The Prisons Department thanked the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) for its swift action and strategic cooperation in tackling the attempted crime.

items.title: Tekun disburses RM36m to 1,500 Negeri Sembilan entrepreneurs since June, says Steven Sim
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 20 — Tekun Nasional has disbursed financing totalling RM36 million to about 1,500 entrepreneurs in Negeri Sembilan as of June this year. Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong said today that, at the national level, RM540 million has been disbursed to 25,600 entrepreneurs during the same period. According to Sim, Tekun, established 30 years ago, has contributed significantly to the development of local entrepreneurs.

items.title: Utility relocation among key reasons behind Pan Borneo Highway Sabah delays
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 20 — The issue of utility relocation has been identified as one of the main factors contributing to the delay in the implementation of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project, said Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam. Joachim who is also the state Minister of Works and Utilities told the Sabah State Legislative Assembly today that the relocation of utilities not only involves the relocation of electricity poles and assets, but also includes telecommunications cables and various other utility assets along the construction line. Joachim said the utility relocation process not only involves the transfer of assets but also includes the verification of the location of the utility, the preparation of designs, technical approvals, the preparation of utility corridors, coordination with the utility owner and the implementation of the relocation works.

items.title: Anwar orders review of civil service promotions after officers wait up to eight years
items.summary: KAJANG, July 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed Chief Secretary to the Government (KSN) Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar to review civil service promotion processes that have been taking too long, with some officers reportedly waiting up to eight years. Anwar said that while the government had implemented salary increases for civil servants, there are still issues affecting their welfare, including delays in career progression. He also said civil servants are expected to work harder, uphold discipline, identify weaknesses and improve public service delivery, but their welfare must also be taken into account.

items.title: Three injured after lorry plunges into ravine following PLUS Highway crash near Bidor
items.summary: IPOH, July 20 — Three people were injured after a lorry plunged about eight metres into a ravine following a collision with an express bus at Km358.5 of the North-South Expressway (PLUS) northbound near Bidor this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said an emergency call on the incident was received at 7.37am before firefighters from the Bidor and Slim River Fire and Rescue Stations were deployed to the scene. “The accident involved a lorry and an express bus, causing the lorry to plunge into a ravine about eight metres deep.

items.title: PJ flash floods: Gobind orders urgent meeting, action plan after repeated incidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Damansara MP Gobind Singh Deo has ordered a meeting involving all relevant agencies to be held today to draw up an action plan following flash floods and a landslide that affected several areas in Petaling Jaya. Gobind said residents’ safety must be prioritised before any repair or mitigation works are carried out. Gobind said he understood residents’ concerns following the latest incident, which raised questions over the safety of roads, river embankments and nearby homes.

items.title: Zizie Izette weeps recalling late husband Bung Moktar as verdict delivered in court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — After seven years of court proceedings, actress Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad was overcome with relief today after the Sessions Court acquitted and discharged her of three charges of abetting in a RM2.8 million corruption case involving investments in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. The 47-year-old actress expressed gratitude for the verdict and thanked the court, her legal team, family and all those who had stood by her throughout the lengthy legal battle. Seven years...Alhamdulillah, the decision has finally gone in my favour,” she told reporters at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex today.

items.title: After 12-year-old footballer’s death in JB, Johor moves to tighten checks on unsanctioned leagues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — All sports events and league tournaments in Johor must obtain advice and approval from the relevant governing bodies before they are held, said the state executive councillor for youth and sports. Johor Youth and Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperative Committee chairman Mohd Hairi Mad Shah said many leagues organised in the state were actually unauthorised because they had not obtained the necessary advice and approval, Harian Metro reported. “There are many illegal leagues being held in this state without sanction from the Johor Football Association (PBNJ) or district associations chaired by district officers,” he said.

items.title: Licensed hunter warns fishermen after giant 16.9‑foot crocodile spotted during Bintulu river operation
items.summary: BINTULU, July 20 — Licensed crocodile hunter Juni Kung has advised fishermen and other river users to avoid venturing out alone in crocodile-infested areas following a recent culling operation in the Kemena River. The other three smaller crocodiles were around 10 feet in length,” he said. “Nowadays, crocodiles often disturb fishermen.

items.title: Sabah targets 1,200MW renewable capacity by 2030, quadrupling current output, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 20 — Sabah aims to almost quadruple its renewable energy generation capacity from the current 307 megawatts (MW) to about 1,200 MW by 2030, with renewable sources expected to account for approximately 37 per cent of the state’s electricity generation mix, the State Legislative Assembly was told today. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the target forms part of the state’s long-term energy transition strategy under the Sabah Energy Roadmap and Master Plan 2040 (SE-RAMP 2040). “The state government’s focus is on the phased expansion of renewable energy capacity through large-scale solar, hydroelectric, wind and biomass projects, supported by energy storage technologies to ensure grid stability and address the intermittency challenges associated with renewable energy,” he said.

items.title: Gobind says Network School approvals followed procedures, investigations must respect jurisdictions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — All applications involving Network School at Forest City, Johor, went through proper procedures and were verified by the relevant authorities, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo. Gobind stressed that business licensing and the operation of the premises fell under the jurisdiction of the Johor state government, while matters involving foreign admissions, visas and immigration were under the Home Ministry (KDN), Astro Awani reported today. “Network School was established in Johor in 2024. Business licensing falls under the jurisdiction of the Johor state government and the school has been operating with the full knowledge of the state government.

items.title: Pakatan tailors campaign strategies in Negeri Sembilan, Loke says each village needs distinct approach
items.summary: JELEBU, July 20 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is tailoring its campaign strategies to suit the unique needs and characteristics of each locality to ensure its message reaches voters effectively during the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, who is contesting the Chennah state seat, said the party recognises that different communities require different approaches, with its campaign machinery implementing strategies based on local demographics and concerns. “The campaign has been progressing smoothly.

items.title: Mohamad Hasan says Asean ministers in Manila to confront Gaza killings, West Asia turmoil and rice security
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 20 — Regional security issues, the conflict in West Asia and efforts to strengthen Asean food security will be among the key agendas at the Asean Foreign Ministers’ Meeting in Manila, the Philippines, which begins tomorrow, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said. We also want to discuss how to achieve Asean food security. “Asean’s rice security is important because people in Asean eat rice.

items.title: Anwar defends KWAP over eFishery loss, says RM12.9b profit puts setback in perspective
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended Retirement Fund Inc’s (KWAP) investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology startup eFishery, stating the losses should be viewed against the fund’s overall strong performance while pledging greater scrutiny of future investments. Responding to questions in the Dewan Negara, Anwar, who is also finance minister, said KWAP’s RM163.4 million investment in eFishery had gone through a rigorous investment process involving professional fund managers, feasibility studies and approvals by its investment panel and board, without political interference. “I chose to announce the investment because it involves public funds,” he said, referring to KWAP’s investment in the Indonesian startup, which is now embroiled in an alleged fraud involving its management.

items.title: Nur Jazlan returns as Dewan Negara deputy Speaker after being reappointed senator
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Former Pulai MP Datuk Nur Jazlan Mohamed has been reappointed as the Dewan Negara deputy Speaker after taking his oath as a senator for a second term today. The appointment was decided by Dewan Negara president Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah after Nur Jazlan was the only candidate nominated for the position, Berita Harian reported. Nur Jazlan’s previous term as a senator ended last month, leaving the deputy Speaker post vacant.

items.title: Kedah JPJ targets vehicle owners who allow foreigners to drive without licences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Kedah Road Transport Department (JPJ) will take action not only against foreigners caught riding motorcycles or driving without licences, but also vehicle owners who allow them to use their vehicles. He said allowing foreigners without the necessary driving competency to operate vehicles in Malaysia could further increase the risk of road accidents. Stien said the enforcement figures covered cases across Kedah, including Kota Setar, with 47 vehicles driven by foreign nationals seized so far.
